name: Red Snapper, Veracruz (Huachinango Ala Veracr
recipeIngredient: 3 pounds Tomatoes peeled, seeded and chopped (save seeds and peel)
6 Whole Chile Largos - canned or Jalepenos en escabeche - canned
1 Sweet Red Pepper - canned or Fresh peeled and cut into strips
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the fish well in cold water to remove any remaining scales.
2. Pierce the flesh on both sides with a sharp fork.
3. Rub the lime juice and about 2 teaspoons of salt over the fish.
4. Place the fish in an ovenproof dish that will just fit it (if the dish is too large, the sauce may dry out).
5. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
6. Heat olive oil in a heavy frying pan.
7. Add onions and fry gently until wilted but not browned.
8. Add chopped tomatoes; strain to extract juice and add with olives, capers, half of parsley, and salt to taste.
9. Cook over high flame until well-seasoned and reduced for about 10 minutes.
10. Pour sauce over the fish.
11. Cover with foil and bake for 25 minutes.
12. Remove foil; turn fish and continue baking uncovered, basting occasionally with sauce for another 25 minutes or until done (fish should flake easily).
13. Sprinkle remaining parsley on top and garnish with red pepper strips.
14. Serve hot with tortillas and/or rice.
